summary_one_line: Input must build XML carrying a default (unprefixed) namespace declaration xmlns="..." so xmlSearchNs hits a nsDef with prefix==NULL and nameSpace==NULL

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An element with a default namespace attribute (xmlns="URI" with no prefix), so a parsed nsDef node has cur->prefix==NULL while the lookup is performed with nameSpace==NULL, and cur->href!=NULL — satisfying the 6182/6183 true conjunction.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile / vpc winning seeds carry the literal xmlns= default-namespace token (e.g. value_profile seed 5 shows lns="1.0 and the naive_ctx seed shows xlins markup); the CMP_MAP distance feedback rewards inputs that get progressively closer to the well-formed xmlns attribute and namespace-URI string compares, so value_profile's corpus accumulates inputs that actually construct a default-namespace nsDef.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and cmplog stay on the false side (L6182 T=0 F=180): their blocking seeds carry only a prefixed/no-namespace structure, so the parsed nsDef never has prefix==NULL with nameSpace==NULL, and execution falls through to the prefixed compare at 6185 (L T=180 F=0) instead of returning at 6184.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback preserves inputs that move the namespace-related CMP operands closer to matching, gradient-climbing the string/structure needed to emit a default namespace decl that naive/cmplog edge-only feedback cannot reward.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed XML document with elements bearing namespace declarations, reachable by recombining generalized token/gap structures rather than blind byte havoc.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves 10/10 with a very high branch hitcount (2199) and its winning seeds are produced by Grimoire structural mutators (seed 2 mutation_op=GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator,GrimoireExtensionMutator) that splice generalized XML fragments containing element/namespace structure, repeatedly reaching the default-namespace nsDef.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline for grimoire) blocks 8/10 with hitcount 2 — without the GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural recombination it does not reliably assemble the nested element structure that holds a default-namespace declaration.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus structural (grammar-free) mutators recombine generalized XML token/gap structures, building the well-formed default-namespace markup that cmplog's havoc+I2S stack assembles only rarely.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same default-namespace nsDef lookup, but reached via xmlSearchNs invoked from the element-start call context where ctxt's namespace stack makes prefix==NULL/nameSpace==NULL the live case.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 with branch hitcount 144 vs naive's 8; its context-indexed edge map records xmlSearchNs edges separately per call path (e.g. the xmlSAX2StartElement caller at SAX2.c:1737), so reaching the blocker under the namespace-search context is rewarded as new coverage and that input is preserved.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 (L6182 T=0 F=180); with a bare edge map the xmlSearchNs edge is already covered from some other call path, so an input that first reaches the prefix==NULL/nameSpace==NULL case is not seen as new coverage and is not retained.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the (call-context, edge) pair at the xmlSearchNs namespace lookup is novel coverage that naive's bare edge counter cannot distinguish, retaining the input that exercises the default-namespace path.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true branch requires cur->prefix==NULL, nameSpace==NULL, and cur->href!=NULL — a default-namespace nsDef looked up with a NULL prefix.
The winner returns at line 6184 (W only) while the loser returns at the prefixed-namespace line 6188 (L only).
At the blocker the winner takes the true side and the loser is entirely on the false side / a different prefixed compare.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L6182  T=735 F=0  T=0 F=180  if ((cur->prefix == NULL) && (nameSpace == NULL) &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Pair 1 (vpc>cmplog) and Pair 3 (value_profile>naive) are both attributable to the value_profile technique.
Pair 3 winner is value_profile over naive on the value_profile delta.
A value_profile winning seed carries an xmlns default-namespace attribute token.
value_profile uses CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback that preserves inputs whose CMP operands move closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: Pair 2 (grimoire>cmplog) is attributable to the grimoire_structural technique.
A grimoire winning seed is produced by Grimoire structural mutators.
grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators recombine generalized token/gap structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire resolves 10/10 with a far higher branch hitcount (2199) than cmplog (2).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=2199  loser=2

evidence_trail.claim: Pair 4 (naive_ctx>naive) is attributable to the ctx_coverage technique.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: xmlSearchNs is invoked from the element-start caller xmlSAX2StartElement at SAX2.c:1737.
naive_ctx resolves 8/10 with branch hitcount 144 vs naive's 8.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=144  loser=8

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2S stage, so the value_profile pairs are not an I2S substitution mechanism.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S lineage query does not apply; I did not run db_query lineage. Evidence rests on the value_profile MECHANISM CONTEXT (CMP_MAP distance-bucket preservation), the decisive-pair deltas, and the xmlns token in value_profile winning seed 5. None of the four decisive pairs is a cmplog>naive I2S pair.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a default-namespace lookup (prefix==NULL, nameSpace==NULL) where value_profile, grimoire, and naive_ctx fail to out-resolve their respective baselines (cmplog/naive) — i.e. all four involved winners block as often as their losers despite the gate being present.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-level link is weak: BYTE DIFF reports no informative divergence, so the xmlns-token evidence in winning seeds is partial and the exact program-side condition is inferred from source plus per-role branch directions rather than a clean W-vs-L byte contrast.
